message.content:
Search the sglang source tree at /sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/ for:
1. GEMM backend selection and tuning - how does sglang pick GEMM kernels? Look for hipblas, rocblas, triton, tuning files
2. Kernel fusion opportunities - what operations get fused, any AMD-specific fusion paths
3. The global_config.py file - what tunable parameters exist
4. Any environment variables or config that affects performance on AMD
5. Weight quantization or processing during model loading
6. Any "is_hip" or ROCm detection and branching logic in the performance-critical paths

Search broadly across srt/, jit_kernel/, and related areas. Be very thorough.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Global configurations"""
2	
3	# FIXME: deprecate this file and move all usage to sglang.srt.environ or sglang.__init__.py
4	
5	
6	class GlobalConfig:
7	    """
8	    Store some global constants.
9	    """
10	
11	    def __init__(self):
12	        # Verbosity level
13	        # 0: do not output anything
14	        # 2: output final text after every run
15	        self.verbosity = 0
16	
17	        # Default backend of the language
18	        self.default_backend = None
19	
20	        # Output tokenization configs
21	        self.skip_special_tokens_in_output = True
22	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens_in_out = True
23	
24	        # Language frontend interpreter optimization configs
25	        self.enable_precache_with_tracing = True
26	        self.enable_parallel_encoding = True
27	
28	
29	global_config = GlobalConfig()
30

1	import torch
2	from aiter.ops.triton.fused_kv_cache import fused_qk_rope_cat_and_cache_mla
3	from aiter.ops.triton.fused_qk_concat import fused_qk_rope_cat
4	from aiter.tuned_gemm import tgemm
5	
6	__all__ = ["fused_qk_rope_cat", "fused_qk_rope_cat_and_cache_mla"]
7	
8	
9	def aiter_dsv3_router_gemm(
10	    hidden_states: torch.Tensor,
11	    weight: torch.Tensor,
12	):
13	    """Use aiter tuned GEMM dispatcher (tgemm.mm) to automatically select the GEMM kernel."""
14	    return tgemm.mm(hidden_states, weight.detach(), otype=hidden_states.dtype)
15	
16	
17	def get_dsv3_gemm_output_zero_allocator_size(
18	    n_routed_experts: int, num_moe_layers: int, allocate_size: int, embedding_dim: int
19	):
20	    if embedding_dim != 7168 or n_routed_experts != 256:
21	        return 0
22	
23	    per_layer_size = 256 * (allocate_size + n_routed_experts)
24	
25	    return num_moe_layers * per_layer_size
26

1	"""ROCm device operations for the SRT platform layer.
2	
3	PyTorch exposes ROCm through the same ``torch.cuda.*`` API surface as CUDA
4	(HIP is a binary shim, and ``torch.device("rocm")`` does not exist). So
5	``RocmDeviceMixin`` inherits all device ops from ``CudaDeviceMixin`` and
6	only overrides identity (``_enum``, ``device_name``).
7	"""
8	
9	from sglang.srt.platforms.cuda import CudaDeviceMixin
10	from sglang.srt.platforms.device_mixin import PlatformEnum
11	from sglang.srt.platforms.interface import SRTPlatform
12	
13	
14	class RocmDeviceMixin(CudaDeviceMixin):
15	    """ROCm device ops — identical surface to CUDA via torch.cuda's HIP shim."""
16	
17	    _enum: PlatformEnum = PlatformEnum.ROCM
18	    device_name: str = "rocm"
19	    # device_type stays "cuda" — torch.device("cuda") is the only valid
20	    # device-type string for HIP devices in PyTorch.
21	
22	
23	class RocmSRTPlatform(RocmDeviceMixin, SRTPlatform):
24	    """Default in-tree ROCm SRT platform.
25	
26	    Capability flags (supports_fp8, support_cuda_graph, support_piecewise_cuda_graph)
27	    keep the conservative SRTPlatform defaults rather than mirroring CudaSRTPlatform.
28	    They are currently only consulted in OOT branches gated on is_out_of_tree(),
29	    so the defaults are behaviorally inert for the in-tree ROCm path. A follow-up
30	    that migrates AMD-specific gating off legacy is_hip() should set these here.
31	    """
32

1	# Adapted from https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/v0.9.1rc2/vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/rocm_aiter_fused_moe.py
2	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
3	# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
4	from enum import IntEnum
5	from typing import Optional
6	
7	import torch
8	import triton
9	import triton.language as tl
10	
11	from sglang.srt.utils import get_bool_env_var, is_hip
12	from sglang.srt.utils.custom_op import register_custom_op
13	
14	_is_hip = is_hip()
15	_use_aiter = get_bool_env_var("SGLANG_USE_AITER") and _is_hip
16	
17	
18	class ActivationMethod(IntEnum):
19	    # This allows interfacing with AITER ActivationType enum
20	    # without importing the ActivationType enum from AITER globally.
21	    SILU = 0
22	    GELU = 1
23	
24	
25	# NOTE: for non _use_aiter case, use lazy registration to avoid overhead
26	# (registration may not be trigger actually, since it will not be called)
27	@register_custom_op(out_shape="hidden_states", eager=_use_aiter)
28	def rocm_aiter_asm_moe_tkw1(
29	    hidden_states: torch.Tensor,
30	    w1: torch.Tensor,
31	    w2: torch.Tensor,
32	    topk_weights: torch.Tensor,
33	    topk_ids: torch.Tensor,
34	    fc1_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
35	    fc2_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
36	    fc1_smooth_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
37	    fc2_smooth_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
38	    a16: bool = False,
39	    per_tensor_quant_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
40	    expert_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
41	    activation_method: int = ActivationMethod.SILU.value,
42	) -> torch.Tensor:
43	
44	    from aiter import ActivationType
45	    from aiter.fused_moe_bf16_asm import asm_moe_tkw1
46	
47	    activation = ActivationType(activation_method)
48	
49	    return asm_moe_tkw1(
50	        hidden_states,
51	        w1,
52	        w2,
53	        topk_weights,
54	        topk_ids,
55	        fc1_scale=fc1_scale,
56	        fc2_scale=fc2_scale,
57	        fc1_smooth_scale=fc1_smooth_scale,
58	        fc2_smooth_scale=fc2_smooth_scale,
59	        a16=a16,
60	        per_tensor_quant_scale=per_tensor_quant_scale,
61	        expert_mask=expert_mask,
62	        activation=activation,
63	    )
64	
65	
66	def rocm_fused_experts_tkw1(
67	    hidden_states: torch.Tensor,
68	    w1: torch.Tensor,
69	    w2: torch.Tensor,
70	    topk_weights: torch.Tensor,
71	    topk_ids: torch.Tensor,
72	    activation: str = "silu",
73	    apply_router_weight_on_input: bool = False,
74	    use_fp8_w8a8: bool = False,
75	    per_channel_quant: bool = False,
76	    w1_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
77	    w2_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
78	    a1_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
79	    a2_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
80	    block_shape: Optional[list[int]] = None,
81	) -> torch.Tensor:
82	
83	    activation_method = (
84	        ActivationMethod.SILU if activation == "silu" else ActivationMethod.GELU
85	    )
86	    # All AITER Fused MoE kernels are expecting the following datatypes
87	    topk_weights = topk_weights.to(torch.float32)
88	    topk_ids = topk_ids.to(torch.int32)
89	
90	    # w8a8 per-channel quantization
91	    if per_channel_quant and apply_router_weight_on_input and use_fp8_w8a8:
92	        # AITER tkw1 kernel for FP8 models with `apply_router_weight_on_input`
93	        # This applies topk_weights on the GEMM output of the first FC layer
94	        #  rather than the second FC.
95	        assert (
96	            topk_weights.dim() == 2
97	        ), "`topk_weights` should be in shape (num_tokens, topk)"
98	        assert topk_weights.shape[-1] == 1, (
99	            "Only support topk=1 when" " `apply_router_weight_on_input` is True"
100	        )
101	
102	        return rocm_aiter_asm_moe_tkw1(
103	            hidden_states,
104	            w1,
105	            w2,
106	            topk_weights,
107	            topk_ids,
108	            fc1_scale=w1_scale,
109	            fc2_scale=w2_scale,
110	            fc1_smooth_scale=None,
111	            fc2_smooth_scale=None,
112	            a16=False,
113	            per_tensor_quant_scale=None,
114	            expert_mask=None,
115	            activation_method=activation_method,
116	        )
117	    else:
118	        assert False, "This should not be called."
119	
120	
121	@triton.jit
122	def upscale_kernel(
123	    A_ptr,  # *fp16 / *fp32
124	    scale_ptr,  # *fp16 / *fp32
125	    Out_ptr,  # *fp16 / *fp32
126	    M,
127	    N,
128	    recv_token_num,
129	    stride_am,
130	    stride_an,
131	    stride_sm,
132	    stride_sn,
133	    stride_om,
134	    stride_on,
135	    BLOCK_N: tl.constexpr,
136	):
137	    pid_m = tl.program_id(0)  # row id
138	    pid_n = tl.program_id(1)  # block id along N
139	
140	    recv_token_num_val = tl.load(recv_token_num)
141	
142	    if pid_m >= recv_token_num_val:
143	        return
144	
145	    # column offsets
146	    offs_n = pid_n * BLOCK_N + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_N)
147	    mask = offs_n < N
148	
149	    # A[m, n]
150	    a_ptrs = A_ptr + pid_m * stride_am + offs_n * stride_an
151	    a = tl.load(a_ptrs, mask=mask, other=0.0)
152	
153	    # scale index: n // 128
154	    scale_idx = offs_n // 128
155	    s_ptrs = scale_ptr + pid_m * stride_sm + scale_idx * stride_sn
156	    s = tl.load(s_ptrs, mask=mask, other=1.0)
157	
158	    out = a * s
159	
160	    out_ptrs = Out_ptr + pid_m * stride_om + offs_n * stride_on
161	    tl.store(out_ptrs, out, mask=mask)
162	
163	
164	def upscale(hidden_state, hidden_state_scale, recv_token_num, output_dtype):
165	    M, N = hidden_state.shape
166	
167	    Out = torch.empty_like(hidden_state, dtype=output_dtype)
168	
169	    BLOCK_N = 256
170	
171	    grid = (M, triton.cdiv(N, BLOCK_N))
172	
173	    upscale_kernel[grid](
174	        hidden_state,
175	        hidden_state_scale,
176	        Out,
177	        M,
178	        N,
179	        recv_token_num,
180	        hidden_state.stride(0),
181	        hidden_state.stride(1),
182	        hidden_state_scale.stride(0),
183	        hidden_state_scale.stride(1),
184	        Out.stride(0),
185	        Out.stride(1),
186	        BLOCK_N=BLOCK_N,
187	    )
188	
189	    return Out
190	
191	
192	@triton.jit
193	def upscale_fp4x2_block32_kernel(
194	    A_u8_ptr,  # *uint8  (view from float4_e2m1fn_x2)
195	    S_u8_ptr,  # *uint8  (view from float8_e8m0fnu), shape (M, N_fp4/32)
196	    Out_ptr,  # *fp16/fp32/bf16, shape (M, N_fp4)
197	    N_FP4: tl.constexpr,
198	    recv_token_num,
199	    stride_am,
200	    stride_an,  # A strides (in uint8 elements) for (M, packed_N)
201	    stride_sm,
202	    stride_sn,  # S strides (in uint8 elements) for (M, N_FP4/32)
203	    stride_om,
204	    stride_on,  # Out strides (in output elements) for (M, N_FP4)
205	    BLOCK_N: tl.constexpr,
206	    OUT_DTYPE: tl.constexpr,  # tl.float16 / tl.float32 / tl.bfloat16
207	):
208	    pid_m = tl.program_id(0)
209	    pid_n = tl.program_id(1)
210	
211	    recv_token_num_val = tl.load(recv_token_num)
212	    if pid_m >= recv_token_num_val:
213	        return
214	
215	    offs = pid_n * BLOCK_N + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_N)
216	    mask = offs < N_FP4
217	
218	    # --------------------------
219	    # Load packed fp4x2 byte
220	    # --------------------------
221	    byte_idx = offs >> 1  # offs // 2
222	    is_hi = (offs & 1) != 0  # select high nibble?
223	
224	    a_ptrs = A_u8_ptr + pid_m * stride_am + byte_idx * stride_an
225	    a_byte = tl.load(a_ptrs, mask=mask, other=0).to(tl.int32)
226	
227	    lo = a_byte & 0xF
228	    hi = (a_byte >> 4) & 0xF
229	    code = tl.where(is_hi, hi, lo).to(tl.int32)  # 0..15
230	
231	    # --------------------------
232	    # Decode float4_e2m1fn
233	    # layout: [sign|exp(2)|mant(1)]
234	    # bias=1, finite-only
235	    # --------------------------
236	    sign = (code >> 3) & 0x1
237	    exp = (code >> 1) & 0x3
238	    mant = code & 0x1
239	
240	    mant_f = mant.to(tl.float32) * 0.5
241	    is_sub = exp == 0
242	
243	    # normal: 2^(exp-bias) * (1 + mant/2), bias=1
244	    e_norm = (exp - 1).to(tl.float32)
245	    val_norm = tl.exp2(e_norm) * (1.0 + mant_f)
246	
247	    # subnorm/zero: mant/2 * 2^(1-bias) = mant/2
248	    val_sub = mant_f
249	
250	    val = tl.where(is_sub, val_sub, val_norm)
251	    val = tl.where(sign != 0, -val, val)  # apply sign
252	
253	    # --------------------------
254	    # Per-token block32 scale: scale_idx = offs // 32
255	    # scale dtype: float8_e8m0fnu stored in uint8
256	    # decode: e==0 -> 0
257	    #         e in [1..254] -> 2^(e-127)
258	    #         e==255 -> clamp to 254
259	    # --------------------------
260	    scale_idx = offs >> 5  # offs // 32
261	
262	    s_ptrs = S_u8_ptr + pid_m * stride_sm + scale_idx * stride_sn
263	    e = tl.load(s_ptrs, mask=mask, other=0).to(tl.int32)
264	
265	    e = tl.minimum(e, 254)  # clamp 255->254
266	    is_zero = e == 0
267	    exp_s = (e - 127).to(tl.float32)
268	    s = tl.exp2(exp_s)
269	    s = tl.where(is_zero, 0.0, s)
270	
271	    out = (val * s).to(OUT_DTYPE)
272	
273	    out_ptrs = Out_ptr + pid_m * stride_om + offs * stride_on
274	    tl.store(out_ptrs, out, mask=mask)
275	
276	
277	def upscale_mxfp4(hidden_state, hidden_state_scale, recv_token_num, output_dtype):
278	    """
279	    hidden_state: (M, packed_N) torch.float4_e2m1fn_x2
280	    hidden_state_scale: (M, packed_N*2/32) = (M, N_fp4/32) torch.float8_e8m0fnu
281	    output: (M, N_fp4) output_dtype
282	    """
283	    assert hidden_state.dtype == torch.float4_e2m1fn_x2, hidden_state.dtype
284	    assert hidden_state_scale.dtype == torch.float8_e8m0fnu, hidden_state_scale.dtype
285	    assert hidden_state.is_contiguous() or True  # stride-based load OK
286	
287	    M, packed_N = hidden_state.shape
288	    N_fp4 = packed_N * 2
289	
290	    # scale second dim must be N_fp4/32
291	    assert hidden_state_scale.shape[0] == M
292	    assert hidden_state_scale.shape[1] == (N_fp4 // 32), (
293	        hidden_state_scale.shape,
294	        N_fp4,
295	    )
296	
297	    # Triton doesn't (reliably) accept torch.float4/float8 pointers directly.
298	    # Use raw uint8 views.
299	    A_u8 = hidden_state.view(torch.uint8)
300	    S_u8 = hidden_state_scale.view(torch.uint8)
301	
302	    Out = torch.empty((M, N_fp4), dtype=output_dtype, device=hidden_state.device)
303	
304	    BLOCK_N = 256
305	    grid = (M, triton.cdiv(N_fp4, BLOCK_N))
306	
307	    OUT_TL = (
308	        tl.float16
309	        if output_dtype == torch.float16
310	        else tl.bfloat16 if output_dtype == torch.bfloat16 else tl.float32
311	    )
312	
313	    upscale_fp4x2_block32_kernel[grid](
314	        A_u8,
315	        S_u8,
316	        Out,
317	        N_FP4=N_fp4,
318	        recv_token_num=recv_token_num,
319	        stride_am=A_u8.stride(0),
320	        stride_an=A_u8.stride(1),
321	        stride_sm=S_u8.stride(0),
322	        stride_sn=S_u8.stride(1),
323	        stride_om=Out.stride(0),
324	        stride_on=Out.stride(1),
325	        BLOCK_N=BLOCK_N,
326	        OUT_DTYPE=OUT_TL,
327	        num_warps=4,
328	    )
329	    return Out

85	def adjust_marlin_shard(param, shard_size, shard_offset):
86	    marlin_tile_size = getattr(param, "marlin_tile_size", None)
87	    if marlin_tile_size is None:
88	        return shard_size, shard_offset
89	
90	    return shard_size * marlin_tile_size, shard_offset * marlin_tile_size

93	def adjust_bitsandbytes_4bit_shard(
94	    param: Parameter, shard_offsets: Dict[str, Tuple[int, int]], loaded_shard_id: str
95	) -> Tuple[int, int]:
96	    """Adjust the quantization offsets and sizes for BitsAndBytes sharding."""
97	
98	    total, _ = shard_offsets["total"]
99	    orig_offset, orig_size = shard_offsets[loaded_shard_id]
100	
101	    quantized_total = param.data.shape[0]
102	    quantized_offset = orig_offset * quantized_total // total
103	    quantized_size = orig_size * quantized_total // total
104	
105	    return quantized_size, quantized_offset
106

108	def adjust_scalar_to_fused_array(param, loaded_weight, shard_id):
109	    """For fused modules (QKV and MLP) we have an array of length
110	    N that holds 1 scale for each "logical" matrix. So the param
111	    is an array of length N. The loaded_weight corresponds to
112	    one of the shards on disk. Here, we slice the param based on
113	    the shard_id for loading.
114	    """
115	    qkv_idxs = {"q": 0, "k": 1, "v": 2}
116	
117	    if isinstance(shard_id, str):
118	        shard_id = qkv_idxs[shard_id]
119	    elif not isinstance(shard_id, int):
120	        raise ValueError(f"Unknown Shard Id {shard_id}")
121	
122	    # AutoFP8 scales do not have a shape
123	    # compressed-tensors scales do have a shape
124	    if len(loaded_weight.shape) != 0:
125	        assert loaded_weight.shape[0] == 1
126	        loaded_weight = loaded_weight[0]
127	
128	    return param[shard_id], loaded_weight
129	
130	
131	def adjust_shard_offsets(shard_offsets, loaded_weight, dim):
132	    actual_weight_size = loaded_weight.size(dim)
133	    target_weight_size = shard_offsets[-1][-1] + shard_offsets[-1][-2]
134	    if actual_weight_size != target_weight_size:
135	        new_shard_offsets = []
136	        new_offset = 0
137	        for shard_id, shard_offset, shard_size in shard_offsets:
138	            actual_shard_size = actual_weight_size * shard_size // target_weight_size
139	            new_shard_offsets.append((shard_id, new_offset, actual_shard_size))
140	            new_offset += actual_shard_size
141	        return new_shard_offsets
142	    return shard_offsets

145	class LinearBase(torch.nn.Module):
146	    """Base linear layer.
147	
148	    Args:
149	        input_size: input dimension of the linear layer.
150	        output_size: output dimension of the linear layer.
151	        bias: If true, add bias.
152	        skip_bias_add: If true, skip adding bias but instead return it.
153	        params_dtype: Data type for the parameters.
154	        quant_config: Quantization configure.
155	    """
156	
157	    def __init__(
158	        self,
159	        input_size: int,
160	        output_size: int,
161	        skip_bias_add: bool = False,
162	        params_dtype: Optional[torch.dtype] = None,
163	        quant_config: Optional[QuantizationConfig] = None,
164	        prefix: str = "",
165	    ):
166	        super().__init__()
167	
168	        # Keep input parameters
169	        self.input_size = input_size
170	        self.output_size = output_size
171	        self.skip_bias_add = skip_bias_add
172	        if params_dtype is None:
173	            params_dtype = torch.get_default_dtype()
174	        self.params_dtype = params_dtype
175	        self.quant_config = quant_config
176	        if quant_config is None:
177	            from sglang.srt.layers.quantization.unquant import UnquantizedLinearMethod
178	
179	            self.quant_method: Optional[QuantizeMethodBase] = UnquantizedLinearMethod()
180	        else:
181	            self.quant_method = quant_config.get_quant_method(self, prefix=prefix)
182	
183	        if self.quant_method is not None:
184	            wrap_method_with_debug_kernel_once(
185	                self.quant_method,
186	                "apply",
187	                op_name=f"sglang.quant_method.{self.quant_method.__class__.__name__}.apply",
188	            )
189	
190	    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
191	        raise NotImplementedError
192	
193	
194	class ReplicatedLinear(LinearBase):
195	    """Replicated linear layer.
196	
197	    Args:
198	        input_size: input dimension of the linear layer.
199	        output_size: output dimension of the linear layer.
200	        bias: If true, add bias.
201	        skip_bias_add: If true, skip adding bias but instead return it.
202	        params_dtype: Data type for the parameters.
203	        quant_config: Quantization configure.
204	        prefix: The name of the layer in the state dict, including all parents
205	                        (e.g. model.layers.0.qkv_proj)
206	    """
207	
208	    def __init__(
209	        self,
210	        input_size: int,
211	        output_size: int,
212	        bias: bool = True,
213	        skip_bias_add: bool = False,
214	        params_dtype: Optional[torch.dtype] = None,
215	        quant_config: Optional[QuantizationConfig] = None,
216	        prefix: str = "",
217	    ):
218	        super().__init__(
219	            input_size,
220	            output_size,
221	            skip_bias_add,
222	            params_dtype,
223	            quant_config,
224	            prefix=prefix,
225	        )
226	
227	        # All the linear layer supports quant method.
228	        assert self.quant_method is not None
229	        self.quant_method.create_weights(
230	            self,
231	            self.input_size,
232	            [self.output_size],
233	            self.input_size,
234	            self.output_size,
235	            self.params_dtype,
236	            weight_loader=self.weight_loader,
237	        )
238	
239	        if bias:
240	            self.bias = Parameter(
241	                torch.empty(self.output_size, dtype=self.params_dtype)
242	            )
243	            set_weight_attrs(
244	                self.bias,
245	                {
246	                    "output_dim": 0,
247	                    "weight_loader": self.weight_loader,
248	                },
249	            )
250	        else:
251	            self.register_parameter("bias", None)
252	
253	    def weight_loader(self, param: Parameter, loaded_weight: torch.Tensor):
254	        # If the weight on disk does not have a shape, give it one
255	        # (such scales for AutoFp8).
256	        if len(loaded_weight.shape) == 0:
257	            loaded_weight = loaded_weight.reshape(1)
258	
259	        # The per-tensor quant-scale must be 1 dimension
260	        if _is_npu:
261	            if param.size() != loaded_weight.size() and param.size(0) == 1:
262	                if torch.allclose(loaded_weight, loaded_weight[0]):
263	                    loaded_weight = loaded_weight[:1]
264	                else:
265	                    raise ValueError(f"{loaded_weight} are not all equal")
266	
267	            if param.dtype == torch.int8 or loaded_weight.dtype == torch.int8:
268	                assert (
269	                    param.dtype == loaded_weight.dtype
270	                ), "init para dtype and loaded weight dtype should be the same"
271	
272	        assert (
273	            param.size() == loaded_weight.size()
274	        ), f"{param.shape=} {param.dtype=} {loaded_weight.shape=} {loaded_weight.dtype=}"
275	        param.data.copy_(loaded_weight)
276	
277	    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, Optional[torch.Tensor]]:
278	        bias = self.bias if not self.skip_bias_add else None
279	        assert self.quant_method is not None
280	        output = self.quant_method.apply(self, x, bias)
281	        output_bias = self.bias if self.skip_bias_add else None
282	        return output, output_bias
283	
284	    def extra_repr(self) -> str:
285	        s = f"in_features={self.input_size}"
286	        s += f", output_features={self.output_size}"
287	        s += f", bias={self.bias is not None}"
288	        return s
289	
290	
291	class ColumnParallelLinear(LinearBase):
292	    """Linear layer with column parallelism.
293	
294	    The linear layer is defined as Y = XA + b. A is parallelized along
295	    its second dimension as A = [A_1, ..., A_p].
296	
297	    Args:
298	        input_size: first dimension of matrix A.
299	        output_size: second dimension of matrix A.
300	        bias: If true, add bias.
301	        gather_output: If true, call all-gather on output and make Y available
302	                       to all GPUs, otherwise, every GPU will have its output
303	                       which is Y_i = XA_i
304	        skip_bias_add: This was added to enable performance optimizations where
305	                       bias can be fused with other element-wise operations. we
306	                       skip adding bias but instead return it.
307	        params_dtype: Data type for the parameters.
308	        quant_config: Quantization configure.
309	        output_sizes: list of output sizes packed into one output, like for QKV
310	                       the list would be size 3.
311	        prefix: The name of the layer in the state dict, including all parents
312	                        (e.g. model.layers.0.qkv_proj)
313	    """
314	
315	    def __init__(
316	        self,
317	        input_size: int,
318	        output_size: int,
319	        bias: bool = True,
320	        gather_output: bool = False,
321	        skip_bias_add: bool = False,
322	        params_dtype: Optional[torch.dtype] = None,
323	        quant_config: Optional[QuantizationConfig] = None,
324	        output_sizes: Optional[List[int]] = None,
325	        prefix: str = "",
326	        tp_rank: Optional[int] = None,
327	        tp_size: Optional[int] = None,
328	        use_presharded_weights: bool = False,
329	        skip_block_quant_check: bool = False,
330	    ):
331	        super().__init__(
332	            input_size, output_size, skip_bias_add, params_dtype, quant_config, prefix
333	        )
334	
335	        self.gather_output = gather_output
336	        self.use_presharded_weights = use_presharded_weights
337	
338	        # Divide the weight matrix along the last dimension.
339	        if tp_rank is None:
340	            tp_rank = get_parallel().tp_rank
341	        if tp_size is None:
342	            tp_size = get_parallel().tp_size
343	        self.tp_rank, self.tp_size = tp_rank, tp_size
344	        assert self.quant_method is not None
345	        self.output_size_per_partition = divide(self.output_size, tp_size)
346	        self.output_partition_sizes = [self.output_size_per_partition]
347	        # If QKV or MergedColumn, use output size of each partition.
348	        if hasattr(self, "output_sizes"):
349	            self.output_partition_sizes = [
350	                divide(output_size, tp_size) for output_size in self.output_sizes
351	            ]
352	
353	        if output_sizes is None:
354	            output_sizes = [output_size]
355	
356	        self.quant_method.create_weights(
357	            layer=self,
358	            input_size_per_partition=self.input_size,
359	            output_partition_sizes=self.output_partition_sizes,
360	            input_size=self.input_size,
361	            output_size=self.output_size,
362	            params_dtype=self.params_dtype,
363	            skip_block_quant_check=skip_block_quant_check,
364	            weight_loader=(
365	                self.weight_loader_v2
366	                if self.quant_method.__class__.__name__ in WEIGHT_LOADER_V2_SUPPORTED
367	                else self.weight_loader
368	            ),
369	        )
370	        if bias:
371	            self.bias = Parameter(
372	                torch.zeros(self.output_size_per_partition, dtype=params_dtype)
373	            )
374	            set_weight_attrs(
375	                self.bias,
376	                {
377	                    "output_dim": 0,
378	                    "weight_loader": self.weight_loader,
379	                },
380	            )
381	        else:
382	            self.register_parameter("bias", None)
383	
384	    def weight_loader(self, param: Parameter, loaded_weight: torch.Tensor):
385	        output_dim = getattr(param, "output_dim", None)
386	        param_data = param.data
387	
388	        # Special case for GGUF
389	        is_gguf_weight = getattr(param, "is_gguf_weight", False)
390	        is_gguf_weight_type = getattr(param, "is_gguf_weight_type", False)
391	        if is_gguf_weight_type:
392	            param.weight_type = loaded_weight.item()
393	
394	        # Materialize GGUF UninitializedParameter
395	        if is_gguf_weight and isinstance(param, UninitializedParameter):
396	            weight_shape = list(loaded_weight.shape)
397	            if output_dim is not None:
398	                weight_shape[output_dim] = weight_shape[output_dim] // self.tp_size
399	            param.materialize(tuple(weight_shape), dtype=loaded_weight.dtype)
400	            param_data = param.data
401	
402	        # bitsandbytes loads the weights of the specific portion
403	        # no need to narrow here
404	        use_bitsandbytes_4bit = getattr(param, "use_bitsandbytes_4bit", False)
405	        if output_dim is not None and not use_bitsandbytes_4bit:
406	            shard_size = param_data.shape[output_dim]
407	            start_idx = self.tp_rank * shard_size
408	
409	            if _is_cpu:
410	                from sglang.srt.model_loader.weight_utils import (
411	                    narrow_padded_param_and_loaded_weight,
412	                )
413	
414	                param_data, loaded_weight = narrow_padded_param_and_loaded_weight(
415	                    param_data,
416	                    loaded_weight,
417	                    0,  # param_data_start
418	                    start_idx,
419	                    output_dim,
420	                    shard_size,
421	                    not self.use_presharded_weights,
422	                )
423	            else:
424	                if not self.use_presharded_weights:
425	                    loaded_weight = loaded_weight.narrow(
426	                        output_dim, start_idx, shard_size
427	                    )
428	
429	        # Special case for loading scales off disk, which often do not
430	        # have a shape (such as in the case of AutoFP8).
431	        if len(loaded_weight.shape) == 0:
432	            loaded_weight = loaded_weight.reshape(1)
433	
434	        assert (
435	            param_data.shape == loaded_weight.shape
436	        ), f"param_data.shape={param_data.shape} != loaded_weight.shape={loaded_weight.shape}"
437	        param_data.copy_(loaded_weight)
438	
439	    def weight_loader_v2(self, param: Parameter, loaded_weight: torch.Tensor):
440	        # Special case for loading scales off disk, which often do not
441	        # have a shape (such as in the case of AutoFP8).
442	        if len(loaded_weight.shape) == 0:
443	            assert loaded_weight.numel() == 1
444	            loaded_weight = loaded_weight.reshape(1)
445	
446	        if isinstance(param, _ColumnvLLMParameter):
447	            param.load_column_parallel_weight(
448	                loaded_weight,
449	                tp_rank=self.tp_rank,
450	                use_presharded_weights=self.use_presharded_weights,
451	            )
452	        else:
453	            # FIXME: This branch is needed to load deepseek v3 awq.
454	            # However, we should fix this and avoid the branching here.
455	            # After QuantizedRL reload, params might still need tp_rank
456	            try:
457	                param.load_column_parallel_weight(
458	                    loaded_weight,
459	                    tp_rank=self.tp_rank,
460	                    use_presharded_weights=self.use_presharded_weights,
461	                )
462	            except TypeError:
463	                # Fallback for parameters that don't accept additional args
464	                param.load_column_parallel_weight(loaded_weight)
465	
466	    def forward(self, input_):
467	        bias = self.bias if not self.skip_bias_add else None
468	
469	        # Matrix multiply.
470	        assert self.quant_method is not None
471	        output_parallel = self.quant_method.apply(self, input_, bias)
472	        if self.gather_output:
473	            # All-gather across the partitions.
474	            output = tensor_model_parallel_all_gather(output_parallel)
475	        else:
476	            output = output_parallel
477	        output_bias = self.bias if self.skip_bias_add else None
478	        return output, output_bias
479	
480	    def extra_repr(self) -> str:
481	        s = f"in_features={self.input_size}"
482	        s += f", output_features={self.output_size_per_partition}"
483	        s += f", bias={self.bias is not None}"
484	        s += f", tp_size={self.tp_size}"
485	        s += f", gather_output={self.gather_output}"
486	        return s
487

489	class MergedColumnParallelLinear(ColumnParallelLinear):
490	    """Packed linear layers with column parallelism.
491	
492	    Similar to ColumnParallelLinear, but the weight matrix is concatenated
493	    along the output dimension. When the weight matrix is loaded, the
494	    different partitions are sharded separately.
495	
496	    Args:
497	        input_size: input dimension of the linear layer.
498	        output_sizes: list of output dimensions of the linear layer.
499	        bias: If true, add bias.
500	        gather_output: If true, call all-gather on output and make the output
501	                       available to all GPUs, otherwise, every GPU will have
502	                       its own output.
503	        skip_bias_add: This was added to enable performance optimizations where
504	                       bias can be fused with other element-wise operations. we
505	                       skip adding bias but instead return it.
506	        params_dtype: Data type for the parameters.
507	        quant_config: Quantization configure.
508	        prefix: The name of the layer in the state dict, including all parents
509	                        (e.g. model.layers.0.qkv_proj)
510	    """
511	
512	    def __init__(
513	        self,
514	        input_size: int,
515	        output_sizes: List[int],
516	        bias: bool = True,
517	        gather_output: bool = False,
518	        skip_bias_add: bool = False,
519	        params_dtype: Optional[torch.dtype] = None,
520	        quant_config: Optional[QuantizationConfig] = None,
521	        prefix: str = "",
522	        tp_rank: Optional[int] = None,
523	        tp_size: Optional[int] = None,
524	        use_presharded_weights: bool = False,
525	    ):
526	        self.output_sizes = output_sizes
527	        if tp_rank is None:
528	            tp_rank = get_parallel().tp_rank
529	        if tp_size is None:
530	            tp_size = get_parallel().tp_size
531	        self.tp_rank, self.tp_size = tp_rank, tp_size
532	        assert all(output_size % tp_size == 0 for output_size in output_sizes)
533	        self.use_presharded_weights = use_presharded_weights
534	        super().__init__(
535	            input_size=input_size,
536	            output_size=sum(output_sizes),
537	            bias=bias,
538	            gather_output=gather_output,
539	            skip_bias_add=skip_bias_add,
540	            params_dtype=params_dtype,
541	            quant_config=quant_config,
542	            prefix=prefix,
543	            tp_rank=tp_rank,
544	            tp_size=tp_size,
545	            use_presharded_weights=use_presharded_weights,
546	        )
547	        self.prefix = prefix
548	
549	    def weight_loader(
550	        self,
551	        param: Parameter,
552	        loaded_weight: torch.Tensor,
553	        loaded_shard_id: tuple[int, ...] | int | None = None,
554	    ):
555	        if isinstance(loaded_shard_id, tuple):
556	            if hasattr(param, "load_merged_column_weight"):
557	                return self.weight_loader_v2(param, loaded_weight, loaded_shard_id)
558	            raise NotImplementedError(
559	                "Shard id with multiple indices is not supported in weight_loader, "
560	                "please use weight_loader_v2 instead."
561	            )
562	
563	        # Special case for GGUF
564	        # initialize GGUF param after we know the quantize type
565	        is_gguf_weight = getattr(param, "is_gguf_weight", False)
566	        is_gguf_weight_type = getattr(param, "is_gguf_weight_type", False)
567	        if is_gguf_weight_type:
568	            param.data[loaded_shard_id].copy_(loaded_weight)
569	            param.shard_weight_type[loaded_shard_id] = loaded_weight.item()
570	            return
571	
572	        if is_gguf_weight:
573	            output_dim = getattr(param, "output_dim", None)
574	            shard_size = loaded_weight.size(output_dim) // self.tp_size
575	            start_idx = self.tp_rank * shard_size
576	
577	            loaded_weight = loaded_weight.narrow(output_dim, start_idx, shard_size)
578	
579	            param.shard_id.append(loaded_shard_id)
580	            param.shard_id_map[loaded_shard_id] = len(param.data_container)
581	            param.data_container.append(loaded_weight)
582	            return
583	
584	        param_data = param.data
585	        output_dim = getattr(param, "output_dim", None)
586	        # Special case for AQLM codebooks.
587	        is_metadata = getattr(param, "is_metadata", False)
588	        # Special case for per-tensor scale to load scalar into fused array.
589	        needs_scalar_to_array = getattr(param, "needs_scalar_to_array", False)
590	
591	        if loaded_shard_id is None:
592	            # Loaded weight is already fused on disk (qkv/mlp).
593	            if output_dim is None:
594	                if needs_scalar_to_array:
595	                    param_data, loaded_weight = adjust_scalar_to_fused_array(
596	                        param_data, loaded_weight, 0
597	                    )
598	
599	                assert param_data.shape == loaded_weight.shape
600	                param_data.copy_(loaded_weight)
601	                return
602	            current_shard_offset = 0
603	            shard_offsets: List[Tuple[int, int, int]] = []
604	            for i, output_size in enumerate(self.output_sizes):
605	                effective_size = (
606	                    output_size // self.tp_size
607	                    if self.use_presharded_weights
608	                    else output_size
609	                )
610	                shard_offsets.append((i, current_shard_offset, effective_size))
611	                current_shard_offset += effective_size
612	            packed_dim = getattr(param, "packed_dim", None)
613	
614	            use_bitsandbytes_4bit = getattr(param, "use_bitsandbytes_4bit", False)
615	            if _is_cpu:
616	                shard_offsets = adjust_shard_offsets(
617	                    shard_offsets, loaded_weight, output_dim
618	                )
619	
620	            for shard_id, shard_offset, shard_size in shard_offsets:
621	                # Special case for Quantization.
622	                # If quantized, we need to adjust the offset and size to account
623	                # for the packing.
624	                if packed_dim == output_dim:
625	                    shard_size = shard_size // param.pack_factor
626	                    shard_offset = shard_offset // param.pack_factor
627	                    # Special case for Marlin.
628	                    shard_size, shard_offset = adjust_marlin_shard(
629	                        param, shard_size, shard_offset
630	                    )
631	
632	                if use_bitsandbytes_4bit:
633	                    index = list(itertools.accumulate([0] + self.output_sizes))
634	                    orig_offsets = {
635	                        str(i): (index[i], size)
636	                        for i, size in enumerate(self.output_sizes)
637	                    }
638	                    orig_offsets["total"] = (self.output_size, 0)
639	                    shard_size, shard_offset = adjust_bitsandbytes_4bit_shard(
640	                        param, orig_offsets, str(shard_id)
641	                    )
642	
643	                loaded_weight_shard = loaded_weight.narrow(
644	                    output_dim, shard_offset, shard_size
645	                )
646	                self.weight_loader(param, loaded_weight_shard, shard_id)
647	            return
648	
649	        assert loaded_shard_id < len(self.output_sizes)
650	        if output_dim is not None:
651	            shard_offset = sum(self.output_sizes[:loaded_shard_id]) // self.tp_size
652	            shard_size = self.output_sizes[loaded_shard_id] // self.tp_size
653	            # Special case for quantization.
654	            # If quantized, we need to adjust the offset and size to account
655	            # for the packing.
656	            packed_dim = getattr(param, "packed_dim", None)
657	            if packed_dim == output_dim:
658	                shard_size = shard_size // param.pack_factor
659	                shard_offset = shard_offset // param.pack_factor
660	                # Special case for Marlin.
661	                shard_size, shard_offset = adjust_marlin_shard(
662	                    param, shard_size, shard_offset
663	                )
664	
665	            use_bitsandbytes_4bit = getattr(param, "use_bitsandbytes_4bit", False)
666	            if use_bitsandbytes_4bit:
667	                shard_size = loaded_weight.shape[output_dim]
668	                shard_offset = loaded_weight.shape[output_dim] * loaded_shard_id
669	
670	            param_data = param_data.narrow(output_dim, shard_offset, shard_size)
671	            start_idx = self.tp_rank * shard_size
672	
673	            if _is_cpu:
674	                from sglang.srt.model_loader.weight_utils import (
675	                    narrow_padded_param_and_loaded_weight,
676	                )
677	
678	                param_data, loaded_weight = narrow_padded_param_and_loaded_weight(
679	                    param_data,
680	                    loaded_weight,
681	                    0,  # param_data_start
682	                    start_idx,
683	                    output_dim,
684	                    shard_size,
685	                    not use_bitsandbytes_4bit and not self.use_presharded_weights,
686	                )
687	            else:
688	                # bitsandbytes loads the weights of the specific portion
689	                # no need to narrow here
690	                if not use_bitsandbytes_4bit and not self.use_presharded_weights:
691	                    # Padding for special case like qwen2_5_VL's mlp which is not 8-aligned
692	                    end_idx = start_idx + shard_size
693	                    if end_idx > loaded_weight.shape[output_dim]:
694	                        loaded_weight = pad_or_narrow_weight(
695	                            loaded_weight, output_dim, start_idx, shard_size
696	                        )
697	                    else:
698	                        loaded_weight = loaded_weight.narrow(
699	                            output_dim, start_idx, shard_size
700	                        )
701	
702	        # Special case for AQLM codebooks.
703	        elif is_metadata:
704	            # metadata indicates fixed size concatenated along dim 0
705	            shard_size = loaded_weight.shape[0]
706	            shard_offset = loaded_shard_id * shard_size
707	            param_data = param_data.narrow(0, shard_offset, shard_size)
708	
709	        # Special case for per-tensor scales in fused case.
710	        elif needs_scalar_to_array:
711	            param_data, loaded_weight = adjust_scalar_to_fused_array(
712	                param_data, loaded_weight, loaded_shard_id
713	            )
714	
715	        else:
716	            ignore_warning = getattr(param, "ignore_warning", False)
717	            if not ignore_warning:
718	                logger.warning(
719	                    "Loading a weight without `output_dim` attribute in "
720	                    "MergedColumnParallelLinear, assume the weight is "
721	                    "the same for all partitions."
722	                )
723	
724	        assert param_data.shape == loaded_weight.shape
725	        param_data.copy_(loaded_weight)
726	
727	    def _load_fused_module_from_checkpoint(
728	        self,
729	        param: BasevLLMParameter,
730	        loaded_weight: torch.Tensor,
731	        output_sizes: list[int] | None = None,
732	    ):
733	        """
734	        Handle special case for models where MLP layers are already
735	        fused on disk. In this case, we have no shard id. This function
736	        determmines the shard id by splitting these layers and then calls
737	        the weight loader using the shard id.
738	
739	        An example of a model with these fused layers:
740	        https://huggingface.co/microsoft/Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct
741	        """
742	
743	        current_shard_offset = 0
744	        shard_offsets: List[Tuple[int, int, int]] = []
745	        output_sizes = output_sizes or self.output_sizes
746	        for i, output_size in enumerate(output_sizes):
747	            shard_offsets.append((i, current_shard_offset, output_size))
748	            current_shard_offset += output_size
749	        if _is_cpu:
750	            from sglang.srt.model_loader.weight_utils import (
751	                pad_loaded_weight,
752	            )
753	
754	            loaded_weight = pad_loaded_weight(
755	                loaded_weight, param.output_dim, output_sizes
756	            )
757	
758	        for shard_id, shard_offset, shard_size in shard_offsets:
759	            # Special case for Quantization.
760	            # If quantized, we need to adjust the offset and size to account
761	            # for the packing.
762	            if (
763	                isinstance(param, (PackedColumnParameter, PackedvLLMParameter))
764	                and param.packed_dim == param.output_dim
765	            ):
766	                shard_size, shard_offset = param.adjust_shard_indexes_for_packing(
767	                    shard_size=shard_size, shard_offset=shard_offset
768	                )
769	            loaded_weight_shard = loaded_weight.narrow(
770	                param.output_dim, shard_offset, shard_size
771	            )
772	            self.weight_loader_v2(param, loaded_weight_shard, shard_id)
773	
774	    def _load_merged_block_scale(
775	        self, param: BasevLLMParameter, loaded_weight: torch.Tensor
776	    ):
777	        """
778	        Handle block-wise scale loading for MergedColumnParallelLinear.
779	        Similar to QKVParallelLinear._load_qkv_block_scale, but for merged column layers.
780	        """
781	        weight_block_size = self.quant_method.quant_config.weight_block_size
782	        block_n, _ = weight_block_size[0], weight_block_size[1]
783	        block_n = 1 if getattr(param, "format_ue8m0", False) else block_n
784	
785	        # Calculate block sizes for each shard
786	        shard_block_sizes = []
787	        shard_block_offsets = []
788	        current_block_offset = 0
789	        for output_size in self.output_sizes:
790	            shard_block_size = (output_size + block_n - 1) // block_n
791	            shard_block_sizes.append(shard_block_size)
792	            shard_block_offsets.append(current_block_offset)
793	            current_block_offset += shard_block_size
794	
795	        if _is_cpu:
796	            from sglang.srt.model_loader.weight_utils import (
797	                pad_loaded_weight,
798	            )
799	
800	            loaded_weight = pad_loaded_weight(
801	                loaded_weight, param.output_dim, shard_block_sizes
802	            )
803	
804	        # Load each shard
805	        for shard_id, (shard_block_offset, shard_block_size) in enumerate(
806	            zip(shard_block_offsets, shard_block_sizes)
807	        ):
808	            # Extract the shard from loaded_weight
809	            loaded_weight_shard = loaded_weight.narrow(
810	                param.output_dim, shard_block_offset, shard_block_size
811	            )
812	
813	            # Calculate per-rank offset and size (considering TP)
814	            rank_shard_offset = shard_block_offset // self.tp_size
815	            rank_shard_size = shard_block_size // self.tp_size
816	
817	            # Load into the parameter
818	            param.load_merged_column_weight(
819	                loaded_weight=loaded_weight_shard,
820	                shard_id=shard_id,
821	                shard_offset=rank_shard_offset,
822	                shard_size=rank_shard_size,
823	                tp_rank=self.tp_rank,
824	                tp_size=self.tp_size,
825	                use_presharded_weights=self.use_presharded_weights,
826	            )
827	
828	    def weight_loader_v2(
829	        self,
830	        param: BasevLLMParameter,
831	        loaded_weight: torch.Tensor,
832	        loaded_shard_id: tuple[int, ...] | int | None = None,
833	    ):
834	        if loaded_shard_id is None or isinstance(loaded_shard_id, tuple):
835	            if isinstance(param, PerTensorScaleParameter):
836	                param.load_merged_column_weight(
837	                    loaded_weight=loaded_weight,
838	                    shard_id=0,
839	                    tp_rank=self.tp_rank,
840	                    tp_size=self.tp_size,
841	                )
842	                return
843	            elif isinstance(param, BlockQuantScaleParameter):
844	                self._load_merged_block_scale(param, loaded_weight)
845	                return
846	            elif type(param) in (RowvLLMParameter, BasevLLMParameter):
847	                param.load_merged_column_weight(
848	                    loaded_weight=loaded_weight,
849	                    tp_rank=self.tp_rank,
850	                    tp_size=self.tp_size,
851	                )
852	                return
853	            output_sizes = (
854	                [self.output_sizes[idx] for idx in loaded_shard_id]
855	                if loaded_shard_id
856	                else None
857	            )
858	            # TODO: @dsikka - move to parameter.py
859	            self._load_fused_module_from_checkpoint(
860	                param, loaded_weight, output_sizes=output_sizes
861	            )
862	            return
863	
864	        assert loaded_shard_id < len(self.output_sizes)
865	
866	        if isinstance(param, BlockQuantScaleParameter):
867	            weight_block_size = self.quant_method.quant_config.weight_block_size
868	            raw_block_n, _ = weight_block_size[0], weight_block_size[1]
869	            block_n = 1 if getattr(param, "format_ue8m0", False) else raw_block_n
870	            shard_offset = (
871	                (sum(self.output_sizes[:loaded_shard_id]) + block_n - 1) // block_n
872	            ) // self.tp_size
873	            shard_size = (
874	                (self.output_sizes[loaded_shard_id] + block_n - 1)
875	                // block_n
876	                // self.tp_size
877	            )
878	        else:
879	            shard_offset = sum(self.output_sizes[:loaded_shard_id]) // self.tp_size
880	            shard_size = self.output_sizes[loaded_shard_id] // self.tp_size
881	
882	        param.load_merged_column_weight(
883	            loaded_weight=loaded_weight,
884	            shard_id=loaded_shard_id,
885	            shard_offset=shard_offset,
886	            shard_size=shard_size,
887	            use_presharded_weights=self.use_presharded_weights,
888	            tp_rank=self.tp_rank,
889	            tp_size=self.tp_size,
890	        )
891	
892	
893	class QKVParallelLinear(ColumnParallelLinear):
894	    """Linear layers for the attention's QKV transformation.
895	
896	    Linear layers for the linear transformation of the query, key, and value
897	    vectors in the attention layer. The weight matrix is concatenated along
898	    the output dimension. The layer is parallelized along the head dimension.
899	    When the number of key/value heads is smaller than the number of query
900	    heads (e.g., multi-query/grouped-query attention), the key/value head may
901	    be replicated while the query heads are partitioned.
902	
903	    Args:
904	        hidden_size: input hidden state size of the transformer.
905	        head_size: size of each attention head.
906	        total_num_heads: total number of attention query heads.
907	        total_num_kv_heads: total number of attention key/value heads. If
908	                            None, assume total_num_kv_heads = total_num_heads.
909	        bias: If true, add bias.
910	        skip_bias_add: This was added to enable performance optimizations where
911	                       bias can be fused with other element-wise operations. we
912	                       skip adding bias but instead return it.
913	        params_dtype: Data type for the parameters.
914	        quant_config: Quantization configure.
915	        prefix: The name of the layer in the state dict, including all parents
916	                        (e.g. model.layers.0.qkv_proj)
917	    """
918	
919	    def __init__(
920	        self,
921	        hidden_size: int,
922	        head_size: int,
923	        total_num_heads: int,
924	        total_num_kv_heads: Optional[int] = None,
925	        bias: bool = True,
926	        skip_bias_add: bool = False,
927	        params_dtype: Optional[torch.dtype] = None,
928	        quant_config: Optional[QuantizationConfig] = None,
929	        prefix: str = "",
930	        tp_rank: Optional[int] = None,
931	        tp_size: Optional[int] = None,
932	        load_presharded_attn: bool = False,
933	        v_head_size: Optional[int] = None,
934	        skip_block_quant_check: bool = False,
935	    ):
936	        self.hidden_size = hidden_size
937	        self.head_size = head_size
938	        self.v_head_size = v_head_size if v_head_size is not None else head_size
939	        self.total_num_heads = total_num_heads
940	        if total_num_kv_heads is None:
941	            total_num_kv_heads = total_num_heads
942	        self.total_num_kv_heads = total_num_kv_heads
943	        # Divide the weight matrix along the last dimension.
944	        if tp_rank is None:
945	            tp_rank = get_parallel().tp_rank
946	        if tp_size is None:
947	            tp_size = get_parallel().tp_size
948	        self.tp_rank, self.tp_size = tp_rank, tp_size
949	        self.num_heads = divide(self.total_num_heads, tp_size)
950	        if tp_size >= self.total_num_kv_heads:
951	            self.num_kv_heads = 1
952	            self.num_kv_head_replicas = divide(tp_size, self.total_num_kv_heads)
953	        else:
954	            self.num_kv_heads = divide(self.total_num_kv_heads, tp_size)
955	            self.num_kv_head_replicas = 1
956	        self.q_proj_shard_size = self.num_heads * self.head_size
957	        self.kv_proj_shard_size = self.num_kv_heads * self.head_size
958	        self.v_proj_shard_size = self.num_kv_heads * self.v_head_size
959	        input_size = self.hidden_size
960	        output_size = (
961	            self.num_heads * self.head_size
962	            + self.num_kv_heads * self.head_size
963	            + self.num_kv_heads * self.v_head_size
964	        ) * tp_size
965	        self.output_sizes = [
966	            self.num_heads * self.head_size * tp_size,  # q_proj
967	            self.num_kv_heads * self.head_size * tp_size,  # k_proj
968	            self.num_kv_heads * self.v_head_size * tp_size,  # v_proj
969	        ]
970	        self.use_presharded_weights = load_presharded_attn
971	        quant_config = None if _disable_hip_linear_quant else quant_config
972	
973	        super().__init__(
974	            input_size=input_size,
975	            output_size=output_size,
976	            bias=bias,
977	            gather_output=False,
978	            skip_bias_add=skip_bias_add,
979	            params_dtype=params_dtype,
980	            quant_config=quant_config,
981	            prefix=prefix,
982	            tp_rank=tp_rank,
983	            tp_size=tp_size,
984	            use_presharded_weights=self.use_presharded_weights,
985	            skip_block_quant_check=skip_block_quant_check,
986	        )
987	
988	    def _get_shard_offset_mapping(self, loaded_shard_id: str):
989	        shard_offset_mapping = {
990	            "q": 0,
991	            "k": self.num_heads * self.head_size,
992	            "v": (self.num_heads + self.num_kv_heads) * self.head_size,
993	            "total": (self.num_heads + self.num_kv_heads) * self.head_size
994	            + self.num_kv_heads * self.v_head_size,
995	        }
996	        return shard_offset_mapping.get(loaded_shard_id)
997	
998	    def _get_shard_size_mapping(self, loaded_shard_id: str):
999	        shard_size_mapping = {
1000	            "q": self.num_heads * self.head_size,
1001	            "k": self.num_kv_heads * self.head_size,
1002	            "v": self.num_kv_heads * self.v_head_size,
1003	        }
1004	        return shard_size_mapping.get(loaded_shard_id)
1005	
1006	    def _load_fused_module_from_checkpoint(
1007	        self, param: BasevLLMParameter, loaded_weight: torch.Tensor
1008	    ):
1009	        """
1010	        Handle special case for models where QKV layers are already
1011	        fused on disk. In this case, we have no shard id. This function
1012	        determmines the shard id by splitting these layers and then calls
1013	        the weight loader using the shard id.
1014	
1015	        An example of a model with these fused layers:
1016	        https://huggingface.co/microsoft/Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct
1017	        """
1018	        shard_offsets = [
1019	            # (shard_id, shard_offset, shard_size)
1020	            ("q", 0, self.total_num_heads * self.head_size),
1021	            (
1022	                "k",
1023	                self.total_num_heads * self.head_size,
1024	                self.total_num_kv_heads * self.head_size,
1025	            ),
1026	            (
1027	                "v",
1028	                (self.total_num_heads + self.total_num_kv_heads) * self.head_size,
1029	                self.total_num_kv_heads * self.v_head_size,
1030	            ),
1031	        ]
1032	
1033	        for shard_id, shard_offset, shard_size in shard_offsets:
1034	            # Special case for Quantization.
1035	            # If quantized, we need to adjust the offset and size to account
1036	            # for the packing.
1037	            if (
1038	                isinstance(param, (PackedColumnParameter, PackedvLLMParameter))
1039	                and param.packed_dim == param.output_dim
1040	            ):
1041	                shard_size, shard_offset = param.adjust_shard_indexes_for_packing(
1042	                    shard_size=shard_size, shard_offset=shard_offset
1043	                )
1044	
1045	            if not self.use_presharded_weights:
1046	                loaded_weight_shard = loaded_weight.narrow(
1047	                    param.output_dim, shard_offset, shard_size
1048	                )
1049	            self.weight_loader_v2(param, loaded_weight_shard, shard_id)
1050	
1051	    def _load_qkv_block_scale(
1052	        self, param: BasevLLMParameter, loaded_weight: torch.Tensor
1053	    ):
1054	        block_n, _ = self.quant_method.quant_config.weight_block_size
1055	        q_size = self.total_num_heads * self.head_size // block_n
1056	        k_size = self.total_num_kv_heads * self.head_size // block_n
1057	        v_size = self.total_num_kv_heads * self.v_head_size // block_n
1058	        shard_offsets = [
1059	            # (shard_id, shard_offset, shard_size)
1060	            ("q", 0, q_size),
1061	            ("k", q_size, k_size),
1062	            ("v", q_size + k_size, v_size),
1063	        ]
1064	        for shard_id, shard_offset, shard_size in shard_offsets:
1065	            loaded_weight_shard = loaded_weight.narrow(
1066	                param.output_dim, shard_offset, shard_size
1067	            )
1068	            rank_shard_offset = self._get_shard_offset_mapping(shard_id) // block_n
1069	            rank_shard_size = self._get_shard_size_mapping(shard_id) // block_n
1070	            param.load_qkv_weight(
1071	                loaded_weight=loaded_weight_shard,
1072	                num_heads=self.num_kv_head_replicas,
1073	                shard_id=shard_id,
1074	                shard_offset=rank_shard_offset,
1075	                shard_size=rank_shard_size,
1076	                tp_rank=self.tp_rank,
1077	                use_presharded_weights=self.use_presharded_weights,
1078	            )
1079	
1080	    def weight_loader_v2(
1081	        self,
1082	        param: BasevLLMParameter,
1083	        loaded_weight: torch.Tensor,
1084	        loaded_shard_id: Optional[str] = None,
1085	    ):
1086	        if loaded_shard_id is None:  # special case for certain models
1087	            if isinstance(param, PerTensorScaleParameter):
1088	                param.load_qkv_weight(loaded_weight=loaded_weight, shard_id=0)
1089	                return
1090	            elif type(param) in (RowvLLMParameter, BasevLLMParameter):
1091	                param.load_qkv_weight(loaded_weight=loaded_weight)
1092	                return
1093	            elif isinstance(param, BlockQuantScaleParameter):
1094	                self._load_qkv_block_scale(param, loaded_weight)
1095	                return
1096	            # TODO: @dsikka - move to parameter.py
1097	            self._load_fused_module_from_checkpoint(param, loaded_weight)
1098	            return
1099	
1100	        assert loaded_shard_id in ["q", "k", "v"]
1101	
1102	        shard_offset = self._get_shard_offset_mapping(loaded_shard_id)
1103	        shard_size = self._get_shard_size_mapping(loaded_shard_id)
1104	
1105	        if isinstance(param, BlockQuantScaleParameter):
1106	            weight_block_size = self.quant_method.quant_config.weight_block_size
1107	            raw_block_n, _ = weight_block_size[0], weight_block_size[1]
1108	            block_n = 1 if getattr(param, "format_ue8m0", False) else raw_block_n
1109	            shard_offset = (shard_offset + block_n - 1) // block_n
1110	            shard_size = (shard_size + block_n - 1) // block_n
1111	
1112	        param.load_qkv_weight(
1113	            loaded_weight=loaded_weight,
1114	            num_heads=self.num_kv_head_replicas,
1115	            shard_id=loaded_shard_id,
1116	            shard_offset=shard_offset,
1117	            shard_size=shard_size,
1118	            tp_rank=self.tp_rank,
1119	            use_presharded_weights=self.use_presharded_weights,
1120	        )
1121	
1122	    def weight_loader(
1123	        self,
1124	        param: Parameter,
1125	        loaded_weight: torch.Tensor,
1126	        loaded_shard_id: Optional[str] = None,
1127	    ):
1128	
1129	        # Special case for GGUF
1130	        # initialize GGUF param after we know the quantize type
1131	        is_gguf_weight = getattr(param, "is_gguf_weight", False)
1132	        is_gguf_weight_type = getattr(param, "is_gguf_weight_type", False)
1133	        if is_gguf_weight_type and loaded_shard_id is not None:
1134	            idx_map = {"q": 0, "k": 1, "v": 2}
1135	            param.data[idx_map[loaded_shard_id]].copy_(loaded_weight)
1136	            param.shard_weight_type[loaded_shard_id] = loaded_weight.item()
1137	            return
1138	
1139	        if is_gguf_weight:
1140	            output_dim = getattr(param, "output_dim", None)
1141	            shard_size = loaded_weight.size(output_dim) // self.tp_size
1142	            start_idx = self.tp_rank * shard_size
1143	
1144	            loaded_weight = loaded_weight.narrow(output_dim, start_idx, shard_size)
1145	
1146	            param.shard_id.append(loaded_shard_id)
1147	            param.shard_id_map[loaded_shard_id] = len(param.data_container)
1148	            param.data_container.append(loaded_weight)
1149	            return
1150	
1151	        param_data = param.data
1152	        output_dim = getattr(param, "output_dim", None)
1153	        # Special case for AQLM codebooks.
1154	        is_metadata = getattr(param, "is_metadata", False)
1155	
1156	        # Special case for per-tensor scales in fused case.
1157	        needs_scalar_to_array = getattr(param, "needs_scalar_to_array", False)
1158	
1159	        if loaded_shard_id is None:
1160	            # Loaded weight is already fused on disk (qkv/mlp).
1161	            if output_dim is None:
1162	                if needs_scalar_to_array:
1163	                    param_data, loaded_weight = adjust_scalar_to_fused_array(
1164	                        param_data, loaded_weight, 0
1165	                    )
1166	
1167	                assert param_data.shape == loaded_weight.shape
1168	                param_data.copy_(loaded_weight)
1169	                return
1170	            shard_offsets = [
1171	                # (shard_id, shard_offset, shard_size)
1172	                ("q", 0, self.total_num_heads * self.head_size),
1173	                (
1174	                    "k",
1175	                    self.total_num_heads * self.head_size,
1176	                    self.total_num_kv_heads * self.head_size,
1177	                ),
1178	                (
1179	                    "v",
1180	                    (self.total_num_heads + self.total_num_kv_heads) * self.head_size,
1181	                    self.total_num_kv_heads * self.v_head_size,
1182	                ),
1183	            ]
1184	            use_bitsandbytes_4bit = getattr(param, "use_bitsandbytes_4bit", False)
1185	
1186	            packed_dim = getattr(param, "packed_dim", None)
1187	            if _is_cpu:
1188	                shard_offsets = adjust_shard_offsets(
1189	                    shard_offsets, loaded_weight, output_dim
1190	                )
1191	
1192	            for shard_id, shard_offset, shard_size in shard_offsets:
1193	                # Special case for Quantized Weights.
1194	                # If quantized, we need to adjust the offset and size to account
1195	                # for the packing.
1196	                if packed_dim == output_dim:
1197	                    shard_size = shard_size // param.pack_factor
1198	                    shard_offset = shard_offset // param.pack_factor
1199	
1200	                    # Special case for Marlin.
1201	                    shard_size, shard_offset = adjust_marlin_shard(
1202	                        param, shard_size, shard_offset
1203	                    )
1204	
1205	                if use_bitsandbytes_4bit:
1206	                    orig_qkv_offsets = {
1207	                        "q": (0, self.total_num_heads * self.head_size),
1208	                        "k": (
1209	                            self.total_num_heads * self.head_size,
1210	                            self.total_num_kv_heads * self.head_size,
1211	                        ),
1212	                        "v": (
1213	                            (self.total_num_heads + self.total_num_kv_heads)
1214	                            * self.head_size,
1215	                            self.total_num_kv_heads * self.v_head_size,
1216	                        ),
1217	                        "total": (
1218	                            (self.total_num_heads + self.total_num_kv_heads)
1219	                            * self.head_size
1220	                            + self.total_num_kv_heads * self.v_head_size,
1221	                            0,
1222	                        ),
1223	                    }
1224	
1225	                    shard_size, shard_offset = adjust_bitsandbytes_4bit_shard(
1226	                        param, orig_qkv_offsets, shard_id
1227	                    )
1228	
1229	                if not self.use_presharded_weights:
1230	                    loaded_weight_shard = loaded_weight.narrow(
1231	                        output_dim, shard_offset, shard_size
1232	                    )
1233	                self.weight_loader(param, loaded_weight_shard, shard_id)
1234	            return
1235	
1236	        assert loaded_shard_id in ["q", "k", "v"]
1237	
1238	        # If output dim is defined, use the default loading process.
1239	        if output_dim is not None:
1240	            if loaded_shard_id == "q":
1241	                shard_offset = 0
1242	                shard_size = self.num_heads * self.head_size
1243	            elif loaded_shard_id == "k":
1244	                shard_offset = self.num_heads * self.head_size
1245	                shard_size = self.num_kv_heads * self.head_size
1246	            elif loaded_shard_id == "v":
1247	                shard_offset = (self.num_heads + self.num_kv_heads) * self.head_size
1248	                shard_size = self.num_kv_heads * self.v_head_size
1249	            # Special case for Quantized Weights.
1250	            # If quantized, we need to adjust the offset and size to account
1251	            # for the packing.
1252	            packed_dim = getattr(param, "packed_dim", None)
1253	            if packed_dim == output_dim:
1254	                shard_size = shard_size // param.pack_factor
1255	                shard_offset = shard_offset // param.pack_factor
1256	
1257	                # Special case for Marlin.
1258	                shard_size, shard_offset = adjust_marlin_shard(
1259	                    param, shard_size, shard_offset
1260	                )
1261	
1262	            use_bitsandbytes_4bit = getattr(param, "use_bitsandbytes_4bit", False)
1263	            if use_bitsandbytes_4bit:
1264	                orig_qkv_offsets = {
1265	                    "q": (0, self.num_heads * self.head_size),
1266	                    "k": (
1267	                        self.num_heads * self.head_size,
1268	                        self.num_kv_heads * self.head_size,
1269	                    ),
1270	                    "v": (
1271	                        (self.num_heads + self.num_kv_heads) * self.head_size,
1272	                        self.num_kv_heads * self.v_head_size,
1273	                    ),
1274	                    "total": (
1275	                        (self.num_heads + self.num_kv_heads) * self.head_size
1276	                        + self.num_kv_heads * self.v_head_size,
1277	                        0,
1278	                    ),
1279	                }
1280	                shard_size, shard_offset = adjust_bitsandbytes_4bit_shard(
1281	                    param, orig_qkv_offsets, loaded_shard_id
1282	                )
1283	
1284	            param_data = param_data.narrow(output_dim, shard_offset, shard_size)
1285	            if loaded_shard_id == "q":

1287	            else:
1288	                shard_id = self.tp_rank // self.num_kv_head_replicas
1289	            start_idx = shard_id * shard_size
1290	
1291	            if _is_cpu:
1292	                from sglang.srt.model_loader.weight_utils import (
1293	                    narrow_padded_param_and_loaded_weight,
1294	                )
1295	
1296	                param_data, loaded_weight = narrow_padded_param_and_loaded_weight(
1297	                    param_data,
1298	                    loaded_weight,
1299	                    0,  # param_data_start
1300	                    start_idx,
1301	                    output_dim,
1302	                    shard_size,
1303	                    not use_bitsandbytes_4bit and not self.use_presharded_weights,
1304	                )
1305	            else:
1306	                # bitsandbytes loads the weights of the specific portion
1307	                # no need to narrow here
1308	                if not use_bitsandbytes_4bit and not self.use_presharded_weights:
1309	                    loaded_weight = loaded_weight.narrow(
1310	                        output_dim, start_idx, shard_size
1311	                    )

1318	            param_data = param_data.narrow(0, shard_index * shard_size, shard_size)

1322	                param_data, loaded_weight, loaded_shard_id
1323	            )
1324	        else:

1330	                    "for all partitions."
1331	                )

1333	        assert (
